name: Acarbose
summary: Alpha-glucosidase inhibitor tracked as a glucose-spike blunting caloric-restriction-mimetic and insulin-axis candidate with robust mouse lifespan and healthspan signals but no direct human aging trial evidence.
mechanism_summary: Blunts postprandial carbohydrate digestion and glucose excursions, creating a glucose-handling intervention axis adjacent to caloric restriction, insulin signaling, and nutrient-sensing biology.

open_questions: Whether mouse lifespan and physical-function effects translate to non-diabetic humans
Whether sex, baseline glycemic phenotype, microbiome state, or gastrointestinal tolerability determine preventive-use value
Whether glucose-excursion blunting is sufficient to modulate aging-relevant insulin signaling in humans
